summary: Updated the repository's dtk-template-derived tooling and CI integration around decomp-toolkit 0.9.6, objdiff-cli report generation, and richer progress/report metadata. The main work refactored tools/project.py to resolve object/library options centrally, add ProgressCategory support, generate report.json as a Ninja build target, support objdiff-cli as a downloaded/built tool, and adjust configure.py to use the new template conventions. CI diff mode was changed to build report.json through Ninja and compare expected/current reports with objdiff-cli. Review discussion focused on the transitional CI behavior caused by checking out commits before master had the new report.json Ninja rule.

key_files.why_it_matters: Largest change. Refactored project configuration/build generation to match newer dtk-template behavior, including Library alias, Object.resolve, ProjectConfig.objects, ProgressCategory, objdiff-cli tool support, report.json generation, and centralized option defaults.

Adopted new template-facing API: Library instead of LibDict, ProgressCategory import/use, objdiff CLI argument/tag/path, updated tool versions, category assignment for game/HSD/SDK/runtime progress, and changed asm/debug/map handling.
Changed CI diff mode to build report.json via Ninja, copy/compare expected and current reports, and invoke build/tools/objdiff-cli report changes.
Added objdiff-cli release URL generation and registered objdiff-cli in downloadable tools.
Bumped decomp-toolkit from 0.9.3 to 0.9.6 and updated hashes/lock output hashes.
Relaxed include and include-guard regexes to accept preprocessor whitespace such as '# include' and '# ifndef'.
Ignored /result, likely for Nix build output symlink hygiene.

what_changed: Bumped decomp-toolkit in Nix and configure defaults from v0.9.3 to v0.9.6.
Updated compiler toolchain tag in configure.py from 20231018 to 20240706.
Added objdiff-cli as a first-class configured/downloaded tool with configure.py --objdiff, ProjectConfig.objdiff_tag/objdiff_path, and tools/download_tool.py URL support.
Refactored tools/project.py so Object instances carry option dictionaries with None defaults and are resolved against library options and ProjectConfig defaults through Object.resolve.
Replaced ProjectConfig.find_object with ProjectConfig.objects, which creates a resolved object map and exits on duplicate object names.
Introduced ProgressCategory and configured progress categories in configure.py for game, hsd, sdk, and runtime.
Added per-library progress_category assignment through Lib(..., category=...) and wrapper helpers DolphinLib, SysdolphinLib, MeleeLib, and RuntimeLib.
Moved debug and map linker flag mutations into configure.py by appending -g and -mapunused to config.ldflags based on args.debug and args.map.
Removed the --use-asm argument and changed asm behavior so asm_dir is disabled unless --non-matching is enabled.
Changed default progress_each_module in ProjectConfig to False, then set it from args.verbose in configure.py.
Changed CI diff workflow to generate report.json through Ninja rather than directly running objdiff-cli report generate.
Changed CI comparison flow to build expected report after checking out base, then restore PR head and build current report before running objdiff-cli report changes.
Relaxed decompctx regexes for #include and #ifndef to permit whitespace after '#'.
smart_moves: Centralizing object option resolution in Object.resolve reduces scattered default handling and makes library-level options, object-level overrides, and ProjectConfig defaults easier to reason about.
Generating report.json as a Ninja target makes progress/report artifacts part of the build graph instead of an external CI-only command, improving reproducibility between local and CI workflows.
Adding objdiff-cli to the same tool acquisition path as dtk, binutils, compilers, sjiswrap, and wibo keeps setup behavior consistent across platforms.
Adding explicit progress categories lets future progress reporting separate game, HSD, Dolphin SDK, and runtime code rather than relying only on all/modules aggregates.
Detecting duplicate object names when constructing the object map makes ambiguous build/progress metadata fail early.
Moving debug/map flag construction into configure.py keeps project-specific linker policy outside the generic template generator.
Using a cargo pool for Rust tool builds in project.py avoids uncontrolled parallel cargo invocations when multiple Rust tools are built from source.

The CI review recognized a bootstrap problem: the base commit did not yet have the new report.json Ninja rule, so transient CI failure was due to the template transition rather than normal project code.
decomp_lessons: When importing upstream dtk-template changes, expect CI to be temporarily self-referential: diff jobs that check out the base branch may not understand newly added Ninja targets until the template update lands on master.
Prefer making reports and progress outputs explicit Ninja targets so local builds and CI consume the same artifact paths.

Keep generic template code and project-specific configuration separate: tools/project.py should provide mechanisms, while configure.py should choose flags, categories, versions, and project library groupings.
Use None as an unset sentinel in object/library options when merging object overrides, library defaults, and global ProjectConfig defaults.
Progress categorization is worth wiring through library constructors early, because it creates reusable metadata for report.json/progress.json without touching each object repeatedly.

Tooling PRs can affect matching workflows even if they do not change game source: report generation, object option resolution, asm inclusion, and compiler tags all influence how decomp work is built and measured.
Regexes in context-generation tools should tolerate preprocessor whitespace forms, not just canonical '#include' and '#ifndef'.
naming_conventions: Renamed the type alias LibDict to Library in tools/project.py/configure.py.
Added ProgressCategory objects with short IDs and human-readable names: game => Game Code, hsd => HSD Code, sdk => Dolphin SDK Code, runtime => Gekko Runtime Code.
Used progress_category as the library/object option key for categorizing progress/report output.
Added ProjectConfig fields objdiff_tag and objdiff_path, mirroring existing dtk_tag/dtk_path-style tool configuration.
Used current="$root/report.json" in CI for the generated current report, with expected-report.json and changes-report.json for comparison artifacts.
Registered the downloadable tool key as objdiff-cli in tools/download_tool.py.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: configure.py now disables config.asm_dir unless --non-matching is set, making asm fallback opt-in for non-matching builds rather than controlled by the removed --use-asm flag.
Object.resolve computes src_path, asm_path, src_obj_path, asm_obj_path, host_obj_path, and ctx_path consistently from source/src_dir/asm_dir/build_dir.
Library and object options now include asm_dir, asflags, extra_asflags, cflags, extra_cflags, mw_version, shift_jis, host, and add_to_all in a resolved option set.
The build workflow now treats report.json as a target built alongside all_source, which can be reused for matching/progress checks.
Debug builds append -sym on and -DDEBUG=1 to C flags, while non-debug builds add -DNDEBUG=1; linker debug output is handled by appending -g to ldflags when requested.
Map generation appends -mapunused to ldflags in configure.py when --map is selected.

review_feedback.comment: A reviewer asked why an extra report file/current-report copy was needed in CI.
Encounter explained the extra file was to restore/keep the artifact upload behavior.

The author questioned whether actual/current could just be $root/report.json after the script rolls back to master and then checks out the PR head, later noting the flow was fixed in commit cbb200bafdea263317c2ba58d954897a77532b86.
The author asked why Ninja could not build the report in both cases and whether the path had changed.
Encounter clarified that the checked-out base commit did not yet have the updated dtk-template support for the report.json rule; the error was a temporary bootstrap issue while adding support.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rely on report.json as a Ninja target or objdiff-cli report changes in CI.
Check whether any downstream scripts still referenced LibDict or --use-asm after this template update.
Review subsequent fixes around progress categories game/hsd/sdk/runtime and report.json output format.
Look for CI changes after PR 1446 that remove temporary bootstrap handling for old base commits without report.json support.
Audit object names for duplicate detection failures introduced by ProjectConfig.objects.
